title: hygrophila episcopalis
intro: Hygrophila episcopalis, also known as Blumea lacera or Polyspora episcopalis, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. Its leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, providing health benefits like alleviating congestion and promoting wound healing. Hygrophila episcopalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for various ailments.
